title: Lifting State Up
description: Share state between components by lifting it to a common ancestor.

slides.title: Sharing State Between Components 🤝
slides.content:
# The Lifting State Up Pattern

Often, two components need to reflect the same changing data. The React way is to **lift the state up** to their closest common ancestor, then pass it down via props, along with event handlers to change it.

This keeps the data in sync and follows the **single source of truth** principle.

slides.title: Example: Two Inputs Sharing State
slides.content: Here, a parent component holds the state and provides both inputs with the same value and a way to update it.

quizData.question: What does 'lifting state up' mean?

options.text: Moving state from a child to a parent component
Using a global state management library
Moving state to a higher level in the file structure

quizData.explanation: Lifting state up means moving the state to the closest common ancestor of components that need to share it.
